text.title: Writing Implementation Plans Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how writing-implementation-plans produces a valid plan artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application: JWT refresh feature
example.code:
--- artefact_id: plan-jwt-refresh owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> version: 1.0.0 last_reviewed: 2026-05-23 feature: jwt-refresh status: Accepted design_ref: .aidocs/features/in-progress/jwt-refresh/design.md ---
# Implementation Plan: JWT Refresh Endpoint
## Tasks
| ID | Title | Files | est_tokens | Depends on | Traces to | |----|-------|-------|------------|------------|-----------| | TASK_001 | Add refresh view + rotation logic | apps/auth/views/refresh.py | 45000 | — | AD-2 | | TASK_002 | Wire URL + integration tests | apps/auth/urls.py, apps/auth/tests/test_refresh.py | 30000 | TASK_001 | AD-2 | | TASK_003 | CHANGELOG + docs | CHANGELOG.md, docs/auth/refresh.md | 8000 | TASK_002 | AD-2 |
## Waves
- Wave 1: TASK_001 - Wave 2: TASK_002 - Wave 3: TASK_003
## Critical Path
TASK_001 → TASK_002 → TASK_003
## Risks
- Token-rotation race condition under concurrent refresh requests (mitigation: SELECT FOR UPDATE on refresh-token row).
## Testing Strategy
- AD-2 covered by integration tests in TASK_002 against the live SimpleJWT pipeline.
## Rollout
Feature-flagged behind FF_JWT_REFRESH; canary at 5% traffic for 24h before full rollout.
## Rollback
Disable FF_JWT_REFRESH flag; old endpoint remains live as fallback for 7 days; revert TASK_002 URL wiring if rotation logic itself misbehaves.
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to an Accepted design; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
